pattern: Precompute row minimums and column maximums, then collect values that satisfy both conditions simultaneously.
hints: A lucky number is the smallest in its row and the largest in its column—compute both criteria independently first.
Find the minimum value in each row and the maximum value in each column, then see which values appear in both sets.
Since all elements are distinct, at most one lucky number can exist; check each row minimum against its column maximum.

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 matrix, sole element is always lucky
minimum in row is not maximum in its column
lucky number is in middle row/column

stress.description: 50x50 matrix with all distinct values 1..2500, some lucky number guaranteed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import random
    m = min(size, 50)
    n = min(size, 50)
    vals = list(range(1, m * n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    matrix = [vals[i*n:(i+1)*n] for i in range(m)]
    return [matrix]
